alkylation reagent: the cysteines were alkylated using iodoacetamide (final concentration 15 mM)
cleavage agent: using three enzymes in order to acquire the optimal peptide coverage for mapping; (1) trypsin (sequencing grade modified trypsin, Promega)
fragmentation method: followed by collision-induced dissociation (CID) fragmentation of 15 of the most intense precursor ions
instrument: mass spectrometric analysis was carried out using an Orbitrap Elite mass spectrometer
ionization type: with ESI ionization on-line connected with the instrument
labeling: Mass spectrometry can be used to de novo sequence monoclonal antibodies but this is not trivial
reduction reagent: the reduction in the di-sulfide bonds of the antibody was performed using DTT (final concentration 5 mM)
reduction concentration: the reduction in the di-sulfide bonds of the antibody was performed using DTT (final concentration 5 mM)
